task_completion.description: Open the DICOM viewer and load the study
task_completion.visual_evidence: A DICOM viewer application (e.g., Aeskulap, Weasis) is open and displaying a medical image loaded from /home/ga/DICOM/studies/multiwindow_ct/.

The DICOM viewer shows different contrast/brightness settings across screenshots, or the window/level (W/L) values in the UI change, indicating attempts to set lung, bone, soft tissue, and mediastinal windows.

task_completion.description: Measure and annotate structures on the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: Measurement tools (lines, calipers, rulers) and text annotations are visible on the medical image within the DICOM viewer interface.

task_completion.description: Export the views as PNG files
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog is visible saving a PNG file to /home/ga/DICOM/exports/, or a file manager/terminal shows multiple PNG files present in that directory.

task_completion.description: Record the survey report in a text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file 'multiwindow_survey_report.txt' being edited, containing the names of the four window presets (lung, bone, soft tissue, mediastinal), anatomical structures, and numerical measurement values.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a graphical DICOM viewer to view the images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a genuine DICOM viewer GUI being used, rather than the agent generating dummy files purely via terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent used the viewer's measurement tools on the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Measurements and annotations are visibly drawn on the image in the viewer, proving the agent didn't just invent numbers for the text report without interacting with the image.

integrity.description: The survey report was created manually using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) is used to write the report, rather than a script automatically generating the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The provided DICOM file is actually an X-Ray Angiography sequence, not a CT scan. Applying standard CT window levels (e.g., W:1500/L:-500) will result in visually nonsensical images. Do NOT penalize the agent for the image content looking incorrect or if the identified anatomical structures do not match a real CT scan. Verify ONLY that the agent used a DICOM viewer, adjusted window/level settings, used measurement tools, exported PNG images, and wrote a text report containing the 4 required window names (lung, bone, soft tissue, mediastinal) and 4 numerical measurements.
